message:
test(readme) + feat: cover align/asof/clip + add AlignMode (fd90.273 / br-6y8kh)

Two related changes:

1. Extended readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover
   7 methods that previously had no integration coverage:
   - Series.clip_lower(threshold) / clip_upper(threshold) — one-sided
   - DataFrame.clip_lower / clip_upper
   - Series.asof(&IndexLabel) → Option<&Scalar> — last known value
   - DataFrame.asof(&IndexLabel, Option<&[&str]>) → Series
   - DataFrame.align_on_index(other, AlignMode) — pandas pd.DataFrame.
     align(other, join='outer') returning (Self, Self) tuple

2. Defect found and fixed: AlignMode (the parameter type for
   df.align_on_index) was exposed at top-level via the existing fp_index
   re-export but missing from the prelude. Same hidden-parameter-type
   pattern as fd90.219/220/222. Added to prelude alongside ArithmeticOp/
   ComparisonOp; extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Land any divergence as [HIGH] review bead with failing input + actual + expected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:23:12.723859876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:49:27.698565537Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T06:49:27.696460643Z","close_reason":"Added 10 live-oracle reshape parity matrix cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity-matrix","reshape"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-6xub","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: add fuzz_rolling_window_with_min_periods target (window/min_periods edge cases)","description":"Series::rolling and DataFrame::rolling expose (window, Option<min_periods>) but no fuzz target exercises the boundary conditions where window > len, min_periods=0, min_periods > window, all-NaN windows, or window=usize::MAX (panic-trigger candidate for arithmetic overflow).

Proposed target: fuzz_rolling_window.rs:
1. Synth mode: 1 numeric Series, 4-32 rows, NaN injection rate from input bytes.
2. window picked as min(N+1, raw_byte) so we exercise window > len; min_periods picked from a separate byte (None / 0 / 1 / window / window+1).
3. Apply each rolling agg in {sum, mean, min, max, std, var, count, first, last, prod, median}.
4. Asserts: output length == input length; all values prior to (window - 1) where min_periods is at default are NaN; never panics for any valid (window, min_periods) combination.

New fp-conformance pub fn fuzz_rolling_window_bytes(input: &[u8]). Stresses the partial-window handling that recent grouped-rolling work (br-x5oy/9uuq/4hkt) only tested at 2-3 fixed window sizes.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: verified parallel agent's commit e909f29 implements the bead spec. fp_conformance::fuzz_rolling_window_bytes (lib.rs:6550) projects byte inputs into a bounded numeric-or-missing Series via fuzz_rolling_series_from_bytes, then runs Series::rolling(window, Some(min_periods)).{sum,mean,min,max,std,var,count,first,last,prod,median}() across 11 aggfuncs — exceeds the bead's 6-aggfunc spec. window_tag modulo 6 hits the edge-case grid {0, 1, len, len+1, usize::MAX, N} via fuzz_rolling_window_from_byte; min_periods_tag modulo 6 hits {None, 0, 1, window, window+1, N} via fuzz_rolling_min_periods_from_byte — covering every edge case the bead names (window=0, min_periods>window, window>series_len, min_periods=0, window=series_len). fuzz_validate_rolling_result locks three invariants: output length == input length, window=0 must reject via FrameError::CompatibilityRejected without panic, and min_periods>window => all-missing output. fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_rolling_window.rs wraps the harness under libfuzzer_sys. Also added fp-frame Rolling::first / last / prod to complete the aggfunc grid (commit e909f29 crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs +46 lines). Tests: 3/3 fuzz_rolling_window_bytes_* seed-assertion unit tests pass under rch (accepts_empty_input, accepts_window_zero_seed, accepts_min_periods_gt_window_seed).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:04.003048692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:24:27.745608554Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:24:27.743158643Z","closed_by_session":"cc-pandas-6xub-verify","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-6y8kh","title":"fd90.273: Cover align_on_index/asof/clip_lower/clip_upper","description":"fd90.273: Cover DataFrame.align_on_index / DataFrame.asof / Series.asof / clip_lower / clip_upper.

Five user-facing methods uncovered:
- DataFrame.align_on_index(other, AlignMode) → (Self, Self) — pandas pd.DataFrame.align(other, join='outer'/etc.) (line 25849)
- DataFrame.asof(label, Option<&[&str]>) → Series — last-known-good lookup (line 26268)
- Series.asof(label) → Option<&Scalar> (line 3037)
- Series.clip_lower(threshold) (line 3713)
- Series.clip_upper(threshold) (line 3718)
- DataFrame.clip_lower / clip_upper (24452/24459)

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with all 7 calls.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 15-20min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T09:14:40.397859881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T09:17:18.710123413Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T09:17:18.708121203Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover 7 methods previously uncovered: Series.clip_lower(2.0) / clip_upper(5.0) (one-sided clips), DataFrame.clip_lower / clip_upper, Series.asof(IndexLabel) (last known value at-or-before — Option<&Scalar>), DataFrame.asof(label, None) (per-column asof returning Series), DataFrame.align_on_index(other, AlignMode::Outer) (returns (Self, Self) tuple — pandas pd.DataFrame.align(other, join='outer')). Found and fixed prelude defect: AlignMode (parameter type for df.align_on_index) was not in prelude — added alongside top-level. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard. All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-6zn1l","title":"fd90.156: add merge_with_options integration test for README Merge: Advanced Options","description":"fd90.74-155 follow-on. The integration test added by fd90.146 covers Quick Example + Quick Start but doesn't exercise the README's 'Merge: Advanced Options' examples (lines 873-902, fixed in fd90.113 to use correct merge_with_options + MergeExecutionOptions + MergeValidateMode signatures). The DataFrameMergeExt trait + MergeExecutionOptions struct are in the prelude but no test verifies they actually compile together.

Fix: add a third integration test case to crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs that exercises:
  - df1.merge_with_options(&df2, &['key'], &['key'], JoinType::Inner, MergeExecutionOptions { ... })?
  - MergeValidateMode::OneToOne via validate_mode field
  - MergedDataFrame return type with .index + .columns access

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas. Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:14:27.880774568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:16:28.782891790Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:16:28.780104763Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
